article_title: Cauldron Ferm Raises $13.25 Million to Scale Continuous Hyper-Fermentation Platform Beyond 10,000-Liter Demonstration

content_preview.title: Cauldron Ferm Raises $13.25 Million to Scale Continuous Hyper-Fermentation Platform Beyond 10,000-Liter Demonstration
content_preview.summary: Australian biomanufacturing startup Cauldron Ferm has closed a $13.25 million Series A2 round to expand its continuous hyper-fermentation platform, which it claims reduces unit costs by up to 50 percent and capital expenditure by 45 percent compared to conventional batch processes.
content_preview.body_excerpt: Cauldron Ferm, an Australian biomanufacturing startup that has developed a continuous fermentation process it calls hyper-fermentation, announced a [$13.25 million Series A2 funding round](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260324502299/en/Biomanufacturing-Pioneer-Cauldron-Raises-$13.25M-and-Earns-Spot-on-Fast-Companys-Most-Innovative-Companies-List) led by Main Sequence Ventures, with participation from Horizons Ventures, SOSV, and NGS Super.
